description_long: This is a World War II-era military fighter airplane, specifically modeled after the Republic P-47 Thunderbolt. The fuselage is painted in dark green with white invasion stripes on the wings and rear fuselage, and it features military insignia and identification codes in white. The nose is marked with a red band and a yellow emblem, and the propeller has four black blades with yellow tips. The cockpit is enclosed with a clear canopy, positioned forward on the fuselage, and the tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The wings are straight and low-mounted, with no visible winglets or dihedral angles. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be modeled in a retracted position.
description: A dark green World War II fighter airplane with white stripes, military markings, a red-nosed four-blade propeller, and an enclosed forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, tail markings, and the red nose with propeller.
description_view_1: Front oblique view highlighting the four-blade propeller, red nose, and the forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the straight, low-mounted wings with white invasion stripes and the overall wing planform.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a four-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration, landing gear not visible (likely retracted).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
